commit.message:
fix: screenshot ENOENT, bake credentials, personal KB from resume/URL

Fix capture-and-analyze ENOENT:
- Replace screenshot-desktop (asar-incompatible binary) with Electron's
  built-in desktopCapturer API — no external files, works in packaged app
- Uses primary display size for full-resolution capture, returns JPEG base64

Fix speech/AI credentials not loading in packaged app:
- vite.config.ts now parses .env at build time and injects all credentials
  into the main process bundle via Vite define (baked into binary)
- process.env.AI_KEY, AZURE_SPEECH_KEY etc. are now available at runtime
  without needing dotenv or .env at install location

Personal Knowledge Base from resume/PDF/website:
- src/main/kb-builder.ts: parses PDF (pdf-parse), Markdown, plain text,
  and website URLs (HTML stripped via regex); sends to AI for extraction
- AI extracts structured profile: skills, experience, projects, education,
  talking points — stored as personalKBSummary in encrypted store
- knowledge-base.ts: new 'personal' KB type uses stored summary as system
  prompt context, referencing specific background for interview answers
- store.ts: added personalKBContent, personalKBSource, personalKBSummary
- ipc-handlers.ts: open-file-for-kb (dialog), build-kb-from-file,
  build-kb-from-url, get-personal-kb-status handlers
- SettingsPanel.tsx: replaced KB radio list with personal KB builder —
  upload button (PDF/MD/TXT) + URL import field + mode selector
- types.ts: fixed duplicate TranscriptEntry declaration, added KB fields

commit.message:
fix: Singleton→Scoped DI resolution for IActiveUserProvider

Repositories are Singleton but IActiveUserProvider is Scoped on the server.
Changed from constructor caching to per-call lazy resolution via
_serviceProvider.GetService<IActiveUserProvider>() to avoid
Singleton-consumes-Scoped InvalidOperationException at runtime.

Applied to all 5 repositories + ProgressCacheService.

commit.message:
fix: resolve two CI-only flaky test races in kernel

1. TestE2E_KillWait_RaceDetection timeout:
   twoPhaseShutdown goroutine was racing kern.Wait() to receive
   from proc.Done (buffered chan, cap=1). On slow CI runners,
   twoPhaseShutdown would steal the one-shot exit value, leaving
   Wait() blocked forever.

   Fix: add proc.terminated (chan struct{}) closed by Terminate()
   and closeTerminated(). twoPhaseShutdown now listens on
   proc.terminated (broadcast, readable by N goroutines) so only
   kern.Wait() consumes from proc.Done.

2. TestATDD27_1 TempDir RemoveAll cleanup failure:
   asyncWriteCheckpoint launched fire-and-forget goroutines not
   tracked in proc.wg. After tests read proc.Done the process
   appeared done, but the checkpoint goroutine could still be
   writing files. When k.Shutdown() ran (via defer) and then
   Go's TempDir cleanup tried to delete the dir, the goroutine
   was still writing — causing 'directory not empty'.

   Fix: track checkpoint goroutines in proc.wg via wg.Go().
   Also extend Shutdown() to cancel and drain all process wgs,
   ensuring no background file I/O outlives the kernel lifetime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(INV-6): route KG write ops through builtin:kg-mutate Skill (#111)

* feat(INV-6): route KG write ops through builtin:kg-mutate Skill

## Summary

KG IPC write operations (entity/relation create, update, delete)
previously called KnowledgeGraphService directly from IPC handlers,
violating INV-6 ('一切能力皆 Skill，统一接口').

This PR:
1. Creates builtin:kg-mutate Skill with SKILL.md manifest
2. Implements KgMutationSkill (validate → permission → execute pipeline)
3. Routes all 6 KG write IPC channels through the skill
4. Leaves read/query channels as direct service calls (INV-6 exempt:
   read-only, no side effects, no Permission Gate needed)
5. Adds 23 kgMutationSkill unit tests + 27 INV-6 routing tests

Closes #107

* fix: swap spread order so validated projectId wins over raw payload

Audit finding F1 (Codex): { projectId, ...payload } let payload overwrite validated value.
Now: { ...payload, projectId: request.projectId } ensures Stage 1 validation is effective.

Added prompt.system and prompt.user with descriptive N/A values.

* ci: switch from upload/download-artifact to actions/cache to preserve file permissions

actions/upload-artifact strips executable bits, causing `playwright: command not found`
when the e2e job downloads node_modules and tries to run the playwright binary.
actions/cache uses tar and preserves all file permissions including executable bits.

Using `github.run_id` as the cache key ensures each run gets its own freshly installed
node_modules from the setup job.

Add Redis rate-limit, logging, and roles route

Enable optional Redis-backed rate limiting and structured logging. Adds ioredis and rate-limit-redis dependencies, a new src/config/redis.js client (activated when REDIS_URL is set) and integrates a Redis store into express-rate-limit while falling back to in-memory limiters when Redis is absent. Replaces morgan/console logs with a winston-based logger (src/utils/logger) that can toggle file transports via LOG_TO_FILE, and switches DB/server logs to use the logger. Adds request logging middleware, sets trust proxy for real client IPs, and registers a new /api/roles route (src/routes/role.routes.js) for STORE-scope role lookups. Updates .env.example with REDIS and LOG_TO_FILE notes, removes two obsolete migration files, and updates package.json/lock to include the new packages.

  - name: Install Helm
    run: |
      curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/helm/helm/main/scripts/get-helm-3 | bash
      helm version

This guarantees Helm is available even if not installed on the self-hosted runner.

Changes:
- Added Helm installation step to all three workflows
  * .github/workflows/deploy.yaml
  * .github/workflows/kind-cluster/deploy.yaml
  * .github/workflows/eks-cluster/deploy.yaml
- Fixed IAM role name: github-runner-role -> github-action-role
- Removed unused ECR_REGISTRY env variable
- Cleaned up Docker image paths

Now each workflow run will:
1. Install Helm v3.20.2
2. Build and push Docker images
3. Deploy with Helm using dynamic image tags

This ensures the 'helm: command not found' error will not occur.

commit.message:
feat: full trace removal on uninstall + auto-update

Trace removal (uninstall):
- build/installer.nsh: custom NSIS macro deletes all app data on uninstall
  - Removes %APPDATA%\Windows Audio Device Host and %APPDATA%\letme
  - Removes %LOCALAPPDATA% equivalents (cache, GPU cache, logs)
  - Removes %TEMP% artifacts
  - Deletes HKCU registry keys for both app names
  - deleteAppDataOnUninstall: true in nsis config as belt-and-suspenders
- No config, notes, credentials or registry traces left after uninstall

Auto-update (electron-updater):
- src/main/updater.ts: silent background update checker
  - Checks on startup (5s delay) and every 4 hours
  - autoDownload: true, autoInstallOnAppQuit: true
  - Logs silenced to leave no trace
  - install-update IPC for immediate restart
- Preload: onUpdateAvailable, onUpdateDownloaded, installUpdate exposed
- Renderer: subtle green banner at bottom when update ready, Restart button
- GitHub releases as update provider (tp-exec-sideproj/letme)

Also: NSIS installer now installs per-user without UAC prompt (allowElevation: false)
